model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Docs link in the navigation
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (308,151) said dx=55 dy=-165 conf=0.90 -> (363,-14)
  iter 1: at (363,0) said dx=0 dy=20 conf=0.90 -> (363,20)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to click the "Docs" link in the navigation bar.
The current predicted click position (red crosshair) is at (363, 20) based on the prompt's history (iter 1: (363,0) + dx=0, dy=20).
Looking at the image, the "Docs" link is centered horizontally around the crosshair, but vertically it's a bit higher.
The red crosshair is slightly below the text "Docs".
The text "Docs" seems to be centered vertically in the black navigation bar.
The navigation bar height is from y=0 to approximately y=90 (based on the yellow line at the bottom).
The center of the "Docs" text is around y=40.
Wait, looking closely at the image, the crosshair is at (363, 20).
The "Docs" text is slightly below the crosshair? No, it's almost on it.
Actually, let's re-examine.
The text "Docs" is in a box.
The red crosshair is at (363, 20).
The text "Docs" is centered around y=40.
So the crosshair is at y=20, and it needs to go down.
Wait, the crosshair is at (363, 20). The text "Docs" is roughly from y=30 to y=50.
Center is about y=40.
dx = 0 (it's well centered horizontally).
dy = 40 - 20 = 20.
Let's re-read the coordinates.
The image shows the crosshair. It's very close to the "Docs" text.
Actually, the crosshair center seems to be slightly above the text "Docs".
The text "Docs" is between y=30 and y=50 approx.
Crosshair is at y=20.
So dy should be around +20.
Let's look at the image again.
The red crosshair is centered on the "Docs" link.
The text "Docs" is just below the horizontal bar of the crosshair.
Actually, the crosshair is almost perfectly centered on "Docs".
Wait, the red circle is around the crosshair.
The center of the text "Docs" is slightly lower than the crosshair.
Maybe dy=10 or 20.
Let's re-evaluate the y-coordinate.
Top bar is from y=0 to y=90.
Center of bar is y=45.
Crosshair is at y=20.
So dy = 45 - 20 = 25.
Actually, looking at the crosshair in the image, it's quite close to the center of the "Docs" text.
Maybe dy=10 is enough.
Let's check x. x=363 seems correct for "Docs".
Confidence is high.
